text.title: OKR Setting Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every OKR Setting application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Spec MUST carry ≤3 objectives; >3 is rejected as over-commitment.
rule.rationale: Beyond 3 objectives the operator cannot focus and execution noise dominates.

rule.statement: Each objective MUST carry 2-4 KRs; 1-KR objectives are rejected (too brittle), >4 is rejected (focus diluted).
Every KR MUST be a numeric outcome metric (e.g. 'free→paid conversion ≥5%'); activity counts (e.g. 'ship 5 features') are rejected.
rule.rationale: Activity KRs reward motion, not progress.

rule.statement: Spec MUST carry quarter start/end dates; ongoing or floating OKRs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Quarter boundary creates the forcing function.

rule.statement: Biweekly check-in cadence MUST be calendared; ad-hoc check-ins are rejected.
